vulnerability.title: Configure Logind to terminate idle sessions after certain time of inactivity
vulnerability.description: To configure logind service to terminate inactive user sessions after seconds, edit the file /etc/systemd/logind.conf. Ensure that there is a section [Login] which contains the configuration StopIdleSessionSec= .
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core && { ( grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real" | sort -VC; } && grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?rhel[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="9.0"; [[ "$real" != "$expected" ]]; } ) || grep -qP "^ID=[\"']?ol[\"']?$" "/etc/os-release" && { real="$(grep -P "^VERSION_ID=[\"']?[\w.]+[\"']?$" /etc/os-release | sed "s/^VERSION_ID=[\"']\?\([^\"']\+\)[\"']\?$/\1/")"; expected="8.7"; printf "%s\n%s" "$expected" "$real"

triage_decision.reason: The remediation only requires editing /etc/systemd/logind.conf to set StopIdleSessionSec= and restarting the systemd-logind service. This is a standard STIG hardening step with minimal risk of locking out users or breaking critical services.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: Potential interruption of active user sessions if the timeout is set too low

execution_details.stdout:
#  This file is part of systemd.
#
#  systemd is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the
#  terms of the GNU Lesser General Public License as published by the Free
#  Software Foundation; either version 2.1 of the License, or (at your option)
#  any later version.
#
# Entries in this file show the compile time defaults. Local configuration
# should be created by either modifying this file, or by creating "drop-ins" in
# the logind.conf.d/ subdirectory. The latter is generally recommended.
# Defaults can be restored by simply deleting this file and all drop-ins.
#
# Use 'systemd-analyze cat-config systemd/logind.conf' to display the full config.
#
# See logind.conf(5) for details.

[Login]
#NAutoVTs=6
#ReserveVT=6
#KillUserProcesses=no
#KillOnlyUsers=
#KillExcludeUsers=root
#InhibitDelayMaxSec=5
#UserStopDelaySec=10
#HandlePowerKey=poweroff
#HandlePowerKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleRebootKey=reboot
#HandleRebootKeyLongPress=poweroff
#HandleSuspendKey=suspend
#HandleSuspendKeyLongPress=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKey=hibernate
#HandleHibernateKeyLongPress=ignore
#HandleLidSwitch=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchExternalPower=suspend
#HandleLidSwitchDocked=ignore
#PowerKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#SuspendKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HibernateKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#LidSwitchIgnoreInhibited=yes
#RebootKeyIgnoreInhibited=no
#HoldoffTimeoutSec=30s
#IdleAction=ignore
#IdleActionSec=30min
#RuntimeDirectorySize=10%
#RuntimeDirectoryInodesMax=
#RemoveIPC=no
#InhibitorsMax=8192
#SessionsMax=8192
StopIdleSessionSec=10min

llm_verdict.message:
The idle session timeout has been successfully configured:

- **Backup**: Original `/etc/systemd/logind.conf` saved as `/etc/systemd/logind.conf.bak`.
- **Update**: `StopIdleSessionSec` set to `600s` (10 minutes) in `/etc/systemd/logind.conf`.
- **Apply**: `systemd-logind` service restarted via `systemctl try-restart`.

Idle sessions will now be terminated after 10 minutes of inactivity, satisfying the security requirement.

review_feedback: QA validation error: Response ended prematurely

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly backs up the file, sets StopIdleSessionSec to a reasonable 10‑minute value, and restarts systemd‑logind, thereby addressing the vulnerability. Minor improvements can be made but the core remediation will work.
concerns: systemd-analyze verify does not validate logind.conf files; it only checks unit files, so this step may give a false sense of safety.
The sed command overwrites any existing StopIdleSessionSec line but will not add the directive if it is missing, potentially leaving the setting unchanged if the line does not exist.
suggested_improvements: Replace the verification step with a manual check such as `grep -E '^StopIdleSessionSec=' /etc/systemd/logind.conf` or test the effective value with `loginctl show-session $(loginctl | awk 'NR>1 {print $1}' | head -n1) -p IdleHint` after reload.
If the directive is absent, append `StopIdleSessionSec=600s` under the [Login] section using a conditional edit (e.g., using awk or ed) to avoid duplicate entries.
Consider using `systemctl restart systemd-logind` (or `systemctl reload-or-restart systemd-logind`) to guarantee the new configuration is applied.
After restart, verify the setting with `loginctl show --property=StopIdleSessionSec` or `systemctl show systemd-logind -p StopIdleSessionSec`.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan correctly ensures the StopIdleSessionSec setting is present, restarts systemd-logind, and verifies the effective value, which will remediate the vulnerability. Adding a backup before modification and using a reload-or-restart command would improve safety and robustness.
concerns: No backup of /etc/systemd/logind.conf is created before editing, which could hinder recovery if an unexpected edit occurs.
suggested_improvements: Create a backup copy (e.g., cp /etc/systemd/logind.conf /etc/systemd/logind.conf.bak) before writing the new configuration.
Consider using `systemctl reload-or-restart systemd-logind` to handle both reloadable and non‑reloadable states safely.
Verify the setting both by grepping the file and using `loginctl show --property=StopIdleSessionSec` after the restart.
Preserve the original SELinux context and file ownership when writing the file.
